channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: O’Leary advocating for 400 percent tariffs on China
item.description: "Shark Tank" investor Kevin O’Leary said Tuesday that he is an advocate for placing 400 percent tariffs on China as the Trump administration ramps up its import taxes on the country.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Ackman: Many small businesses will go bankrupt if Trump doesn't pause tariffs soon
item.description: Billionaire hedge fund investor Bill Ackman suggested small businesses will be crippled by President Trump’s sweeping tariffs if a pause on global import taxes isn't implemented by the administration.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: US restores urgent food aid but not in Afghanistan and Yemen, where millions need it
item.description: United Nations officials say the Trump administration has reversed sweeping cuts in emergency food aid to several nations but maintained them in Afghanistan and Yemen.
item.dc:creator: Samy Magdy, Riazat Butt And Ellen Knickmeyer, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: 8 people die from cholera in South Sudan as funding cuts force longer walks to clinics
item.description: An aid organization says five children are among eight people who have died from cholera in South Sudan after aid cuts are forcing patients to walk for hours to reach the nearest clinics.
item.dc:creator: Evelyne Musambi, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Long, fraught timeline of tensions between Iran and the US as nuclear negotiations approach
item.description: Iran and the United States will hold talks in the sultanate of Oman on Saturday in an attempt to jump-start negotiations over Tehran’s rapidly advancing nuclear program.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: German parties agree to form a new government after months of drift in Europe's biggest economy
item.description: German election winner Friedrich Merz has sealed a deal to form a new government that aims to spur economic growth, ramp up defense spending, take a tougher approach to migration and catch up on long-neglected modernization.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Iran's president insists Tehran does not seek a nuclear bomb and dangles US business opportunities
item.description: Iran’s president again has pledged his nation is “not after a nuclear bomb” ahead of talks between Tehran and the United States.
item.dc:creator: Nasser Karimi And Jon Gambrell, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Beijing rejects Ukraine's claim significant numbers of Chinese troops are fighting alongside Russia
item.description: Ukraine’s claim that significant numbers of Chinese nationals are fighting alongside Russia’s invading army is “totally unfounded,” a Beijing official said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Ukraine's Zelenskyy says 2 Chinese were caught fighting alongside Russia
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — The Ukrainian military has captured two Chinese men fighting alongside the Russian army in the eastern Donetsk region and has information that “significantly more” are with Russian forces, Ukraine’s President Volodymyr Zelenskyy announced on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: VASILISA STEPANENKO, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: US defense secretary says China is a threat to Panama Canal, spurring a fiery response from Beijing
item.description: PANAMA CITY (AP) — U.S. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said Tuesday the Panama Canal faces ongoing threats from China but that together the United States and Panama will keep it secure.
item.dc:creator: JUAN ZAMORANO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What to know about the roof collapse at the Jet Set club in the Dominican Republic
item.description: The roof of an iconic nightclub in the capital of the Dominican Republic collapsed nearly an hour after a merengue concert began, killing at least 113 people and injuring at least 255.
item.dc:creator: Dánica Coto, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Iran's foreign minister says he will have indirect talks with US envoy over Tehran's nuclear program
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Iran 's foreign minister said Tuesday he'll meet with U.S. envoy Steve Witkoff in Oman for the first negotiations under the Trump administration seeking to halt Tehran’s rapidly advancing nuclear program as tensions remain high in the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL and AMIR VAHDAT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Korea says 10 North Korean soldiers crossed military demarcation line, warning shots fired
item.description: South Korea's military said troops aired warning broadcasts and fired warning shots after approximately 10 North Korean soldiers crossed the military demarcation line in the demilitarized zone.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A US-Russian crew of 3 arrives at the International Space Station
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — NASA astronaut Jonny Kim and two Russian crewmates arrived at the International Space Station on Tuesday on board a Russian spacecraft.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Yemen's Houthis say US strikes kill 6 as Trump's bombing video suggests higher overall death toll
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Suspected U.S. airstrikes over the weekend targeting Yemen's Houthi rebels killed at least six people, the group said Sunday, while a bombing video posted by U.S.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: US revokes visas for South Sudanese as civil war looms at home
item.description: Here’s a look at South Sudan, whose people had been granted temporary protected status by the U.S. because of insecurity at home. That status expires on May 3.
item.dc:creator: CARA ANNA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Pope makes surprise appearance at St. Peter’s Square, 2 weeks after leaving hospital
item.description: VATICAN CITY (AP) — Pope Francis made a surprise entrance to St. Peter’s Square during a special Jubilee Mass for the sick and health workers on Sunday, marking his first public appearance at the Vatican since leaving t he hospital two weeks ago after a life-threatening bout with pneumonia.
item.dc:creator: SILVIA STELLACCI and COLLEEN BARRY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Hamas terror outlet quietly cuts Gaza death count, reveals most killed were combat-age men
item.description: New analysis shows thousands of names were removed from the Gaza casualty list as Hamas terror organization admits most deaths aged between 13 to 55 were males.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russian air strikes kill 1 in Kyiv as Zelenskyy demands more pressure on Putin
item.description: One person was killed in Russian air strikes on Kyiv on Sunday while Kryvyi Rih mourns 19 victims of Friday's attack, which local authorities say also destroyed dozens of homes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope makes surprise appearance at St. Peter’s Square, 2 weeks after leaving hospital
item.description: Pope Francis made a surprise entrance to St. Peter’s Square during a special Jubilee Mass for the sick and health workers on Sunday, marking his first public appearance at the Vatican since leaving t he hospital two weeks ago after a life-threatening bout with pneumonia.
item.dc:creator: Silvia Stellacci, WFLA, NBC

item.title: UN global comms arm under fire for anti-Israel bias as critics call for reforms
item.description: The U.N.'s Department of Global Communications is under fire for a one-sided view of the Israel-Palestinian conflict, with critics saying it often sides with Palestinians at the expense of other conflicts.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: JD Vance's warning on Europe's future shines spotlight on continent's growing list of problems
item.description: Many countries in Europe are struggling to come to terms with mass migration, rising crime and weak economies. But some nations have taken action and are seeing hopeful results.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: UN official reappointed despite accusations of antisemitism
item.description: Allegations of antisemitism and the condemnation of several nations did not stop Special Rapporteur Francesca Albanese from securing another three-year term in her position.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New ecosystem found after Chicago-sized chunk of Antarctica breaks off
item.description: They found animal and marine life as deep as nearly 4,300 feet. The species in the newly discovered ecosystem include crustaceans, sponges, corals and octopus.
item.dc:creator: Ashley N. Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: South Korea presidential ouster part of Chinese strategy to 'expand its regional influence,' expert says
item.description: One analyst, as well as supporters of South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol, says China backed Yoon's ouster in South Korea to weaken U.S. ties and expand its influence by propping up Beijing-friendly political allies.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: China slaps a 34% tax on all US imports in retaliation for Trump's tariffs
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — China announced Friday that it will impose a 34% tax on all U.S. imports next week, part of a flurry of retaliatory measures to U.S. President Donald Trump’s new tariffs that delivered the strongest response yet from Beijing to the American leader's trade war.
item.dc:creator: HUIZHONG WU, ELAINE KURTENBACH and DIDI TANG, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: India's parliament passes bill that would change Muslim land endowments
item.description: India's parliament passes a bill amending Muslim land endowments, sparking protests over concerns it undermines minority rights and could lead to property confiscation.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Officials say a Russian strike in central Ukraine kills 14 people, including six children
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — A Russian ballistic missile strike Friday on a central Ukrainian city killed at least 14 people, including six children, Ukrainian officials said, as U.S. and European leaders pressed Russia to accept a ceasefire in the conflict.
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA and ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Luxury cruise ship caught in 40-foot waves while traveling through Drake Passage
item.description: Passengers on Quark Expeditions' Ocean Explorer ship got rocked with waves up to 40 feet on a recent cruise, rocking those onboard.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israeli strike on a school in Gaza kills at least 27 people, Palestinian health officials say
item.description: DEIR AL BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Israeli airstrikes killed at least 100 Palestinians across the Gaza Strip on Thursday, including 27 or more sheltering at a school, according to Palestinian medical authorities, in a stepped-up offensive that Israel’s military said is intended to pressure Hamas and e
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and NATALIE MELZER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: White House defends not including Russia, North Korea on tariffs
item.description: The official said that President Trump has “recently threatened to impose strong sanctions on Russia” to further explain leaving out Moscow.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation
